name: QuiltFit: Quilt Design Planner
description:
QuiltFit is a focused quilt planning workspace for quilters who want to move from idea to fabric without opening a spreadsheet. Start with a block pattern, choose your fabrics, preview the full quilt, estimate yardage, build a shopping list, create a cutting plan and follow the sewing sequence from the same quiet app.

It is made for patchwork beginners, weekend makers, guild quilters, sewing teachers and anyone who wants a clearer path from "I love this pattern" to "I know what to buy, cut and sew." QuiltFit keeps the workflow practical: design first, then fabric requirements, then cutting, then piecing.

Design your quilt visually
Choose a classic block pattern such as Nine Patch, Log Cabin, Half-Square Triangle or Flying Geese. Set the quilt width and height in blocks, pick a finished block size and see the finished size update as you work. The quilt preview shows how the block repeats across the whole project, so color balance and contrast are easier to judge before you cut into real fabric.

Add fabrics you actually own or plan to buy. Use built-in colors and textures, create custom fabrics, name them, and assign them to the block. Paint a single cell, fill a role, or use Magic Fill to quickly explore a layout. QuiltFit is meant to help you audition ideas quickly, whether you are working with cream and navy, scraps from your stash, seasonal prints, solids, florals, plaids or a simple two-color design.

Fabric planning and shopping list
Once fabrics are assigned, QuiltFit calculates practical yardage estimates for each fabric based on the project size, block size, seam allowance and safety margin. The shopping list turns those estimates into a clear checklist, with fabric names, colors, quantities and optional prices. Mark items as bought, share the list, or bring it to the fabric shop so you do not have to rebuild the math at the counter.

Cutting made less confusing
QuiltFit turns the design into a cut list with piece counts and cut sizes. Use the simple list when you want a fast checklist, or the visual cutting view when you want to see the work grouped by fabric. Export a printable PDF cut list with fabric requirements and piece sizes. The goal is not to replace your judgement at the cutting table, but to give you a clean plan you can check, print and follow.

Sewing guidance block by block
After shopping and cutting, QuiltFit helps you move into sewing. Illustrated steps break the selected block into smaller actions: cut pieces, arrange units, sew seams, press, assemble rows and complete blocks. Track progress across the blocks in your quilt top so the project feels manageable instead of scattered across notes, photos and memory.

Project library and settings
Save multiple quilts in the project library, switch between them and keep each design's fabrics, size, progress and settings together. Adjust seam allowance, safety margin, block size, project status and language from the settings screen. QuiltFit is designed as a compact planning notebook for the quilting work that happens before and during sewing.

Privacy built into the workflow
QuiltFit is built around five practical promises:

Offline: core planning tools are available without relying on a web account.
No Login: open the app and design, calculate, cut and sew without creating an account.
Privacy First: quilt names, fabric choices, recipient notes and project details are treated as personal workspace data.
On Device: project planning, yardage, cut lists, PDFs, settings and progress are handled on your device.
No Cloud Upload: QuiltFit does not upload your quilt projects, fabric lists, shopping lists, cut plans or sewing progress to its own cloud.

name: Work Shift Schedule Calendar
description:
Work Shift Schedule Calendar takes the guesswork out of rotating shifts, relay schedules and fixed work-and-off patterns. If you constantly find yourself asking "am I working today or is this my day off?", or scrolling through old messages and screenshots trying to remember your roster, this app gives you a clear, always up-to-date shift calendar in seconds — without an account, without the cloud, and without sharing your schedule with anyone you don't choose to.

Solve the daily "what shift is it" problem
Shift workers, factory and plant operators, nurses, security staff, drivers, warehouse and retail teams, and anyone on a rotating roster know the feeling: the schedule lives in someone else's spreadsheet, a printed sheet on a wall, or a group chat photo that's already out of date. Work Shift Schedule Calendar puts your roster on your phone, generated automatically from the rotation pattern you actually work, so you can see today's shift, tomorrow's shift, and your next day off at a glance.

Build your schedule in seconds
Pick your rotation type — common patterns like 6x2, 5x2, 4x2, 3x1, 2x1, 1x1, 5x1 and 4x1 are built in — set the date of your first day off, and the app instantly generates your full calendar from that anchor date. No manual entry, no spreadsheets, no formulas. Switch rotations any time your roster changes.

Color-coded day and night shifts
Day and night shifts are tagged with distinct colors so your calendar is easy to scan at a glance, helping you spot upcoming days off without tapping into every date.

Plan your life around your days off
Want to plan dinner, a trip, or a family visit but keep checking your roster first? See your upcoming days off in a clean monthly or annual view, so you can commit to plans with confidence.

Compare schedules to find shared days off
The Compare tool lines up two different rotations side by side, so you and a partner, friend, or colleague on a different roster can quickly find days you're both off. Perfect for couples on different shifts, coworkers planning a swap, or friends scheduling a meetup.

Track hours, overtime and estimated pay
Not sure your paycheck is right? Set your hours per shift, hourly rate and overtime multiplier, and the app automatically tracks total work hours, overtime hours and estimated pay from your generated schedule.

Smart reminders so you never miss a shift
Get a reminder before a work day, a heads-up the evening before a day off, a morning notification on days off, and a Monday overview of the week ahead.

Export and share your schedule
Save your monthly schedule as an image to share in a group chat, or export it as CSV — useful for swapping shifts, requesting time off, or keeping a personal log.

Make it yours
Choose your accent color, set whether your week starts on Monday, show or hide week numbers, and switch the in-app language independently from your device language.

Privacy by design
Your work schedule, pay details and routine say a lot about your life, so this app is built around one rule: your data stays yours.

Offline: build, view and edit your schedule, hours, pay estimates and reminders without an internet connection.
No Login: open the app and start scheduling immediately — no account, no sign-up, no password.
Privacy First: your rotation, shift history, hours and pay are treated as private information, never as data to collect.
On Device: schedule generation, hour and pay calculations, and reminders are processed locally on your iPhone.
No Cloud Upload: your schedule, hours, pay settings and exported files are never uploaded to any server.

Designed for privacy-focused users, Priviscribe keeps your recordings and transcripts on your device. There is no account required, no server upload, and no need to send sensitive audio to external platforms. Whether you are documenting business meetings, recording class discussions, transcribing interviews, or saving personal voice notes, Priviscribe gives you a secure and simple way to convert speech into text.

Key Features

• Offline Speech to Text  
Transcribe audio directly on your device with no internet required for core transcription workflows. Your voice recordings and transcripts stay private and under your control.

• Real-Time Transcription  
See text appear while recording so you can follow conversations, review spoken content faster, and capture meeting notes as they happen.

• Speaker Labeling for Meetings and Interviews  
Priviscribe can separate different speakers in conversations, making it easier to understand who said what during meetings, interviews, discussions, and group sessions.

• Rename and Organize Speakers  
Edit speaker names for cleaner transcripts and more readable meeting notes. This is especially useful for team meetings, research calls, class discussions, and interview recordings.

• Private Meeting Notes  
Capture meeting discussions and convert them into searchable text without sending confidential company conversations to the cloud.

• Lecture and Study Transcripts  
Record lessons, lectures, seminars, and study sessions, then review transcripts later for note-taking, summaries, and revision.

• Interview Transcription  
Useful for journalists, researchers, students, creators, and professionals who need fast voice transcription with speaker separation.

• Voice Notes and Idea Capture  
Quickly record ideas, reminders, brainstorming sessions, and personal thoughts, then turn them into text you can save, copy, or share.

• Export and Share  
Copy transcripts, save text, and share notes to your favorite apps, email, or productivity tools for follow-up and collaboration.

• Adjustable Accuracy and Performance  
Choose settings that fit your needs, whether you want faster processing, balanced performance, or higher transcription quality on supported devices.

Why Choose Priviscribe?

Priviscribe is more than a simple voice recorder. It combines offline speech recognition, speaker labeled transcripts, and privacy-first processing in one streamlined app. Many transcription apps rely on cloud uploads, but Priviscribe is designed for users who want more control over sensitive recordings and meeting content.

It is ideal for:
• Business meetings and work conversations
• Interviews and research recordings
• Lecture notes and class discussions
• Study sessions and revision
• Personal voice memos and thought capture
• Team collaboration and meeting follow-up
• Private conversations that should remain on device

Privacy First

Your privacy matters. Priviscribe is built around on-device processing so your audio and transcripts do not need to leave your device for transcription. No sign-up. No unnecessary data collection. No cloud dependency for the core experience.

If you need an offline speech to text app for private meeting notes, interview transcription, lecture recording, speaker labeled transcripts, and secure voice to text workflows, Priviscribe is built for you.

name: Cadenza: Metronome & Tuner
description:
Cadenza is a focused metronome, tuner and practice companion for musicians who want to open one app and start working. It is made for daily practice, lesson warmups, ensemble rehearsals, auditions, choir preparation, studio sessions and quiet at-home repetition. The interface stays calm: a large BPM display, clear controls, a responsive tuner, setlist notes and a practice log without account setup getting in the way.

Cadenza is built around five practical promises: Offline, No Login, Privacy First, On Device, No Cloud Upload. An active subscription is required to use Cadenza, but you do not need to create an app account. Tempo choices, tuning work, setlist details and practice history are meant to stay on your iPhone. Cadenza does not turn a rehearsal into a cloud workspace, and it does not require uploading audio or practice notes just to tune, count or keep time.

Metronome for real practice
Set a tempo from 20 to 240 BPM, nudge it one beat at a time, or move quickly with the slider. The big number is readable from a music stand, piano bench, desk or rehearsal room floor. Tempo names such as Largo, Andante, Allegro, Presto and Prestissimo give quick musical context instead of leaving you with only a number. Tap Tempo lets you tap the beat you feel and turn it into a BPM value when you are learning from a recording, matching a conductor, or finding the natural pace of a phrase.

Common time signatures are ready, including 2/4, 3/4, 4/4, 5/8, 6/8, 7/8, 9/8, 11/8 and 12/8. Beat dots show the shape of the bar. Tap a dot to cycle accents from strong to medium, weak or silent, so you can make simple bars, compound meters, odd meters, subdivision drills and accent studies feel closer to the music you are actually practicing.

Tempo Ramp helps build speed with control. Choose a starting BPM, an ending BPM and how many bars the ramp should take. Use it for scales, etudes, double-tonguing, picking exercises, bowing patterns, rhythm studies or any passage that needs gradual speed rather than a sudden jump. The goal is steady progress, not guessing whether the tempo changed too fast.

Tuner and reference tones
The tuner listens with the microphone and shows the nearest note, frequency and cents offset. Use the chromatic mode for voice, winds, brass, strings, percussion tuning references or any pitch you want to check. Instrument presets for guitar, bass guitar, ukulele, violin and cello make it easier to think in familiar strings. A4 reference choices include 432, 440, 441, 442, 443 and 444 Hz, useful for different ensembles, teachers and rehearsal standards.

Cadenza also includes drone tones. Play a sustained reference note when you want to tune by ear, check intonation, practice long tones, settle a scale, hear intervals against a root, or train your pitch center without staring at a needle the whole time.

Setlist for rehearsal memory
Save pieces with title, composer, BPM, time signature, key and notes. Load a piece directly into the metronome when rehearsal moves quickly. This is useful for bands, choirs, worship sets, orchestra excerpts, theater pits, lesson plans and personal routines where every piece has a different tempo or meter.

Offline: core tools are available for everyday practice without relying on a web account.
No Login: open the app and use the metronome, tuner, setlist and practice log without sign-in.
Privacy First: the product is designed around minimal data needs and clear local workflows.
On Device: tuning, tempo work, setlists, settings and practice history are handled on your device.
No Cloud Upload: Cadenza does not upload your tuner audio, metronome use, setlists or practice logs to its own cloud.

When it is time to prepare a report, Expense Report Maker gives you practical export options. Filter expenses by month, project, category, or a custom date range. Add report details such as title, submitter name, company name, client or project name, and report notes. Export a summary PDF for quick reimbursement, a detailed PDF that includes receipt images, or a CSV file that can be opened in spreadsheet apps and accounting workflows.

Key features:
• Fast manual expense entry
• Receipt photo attachment from camera or photo library
• OCR-assisted receipt recognition for amount, merchant, date, and category
• Month, project, and category filters
• Category totals and overall total on the expense list
• Custom projects for clients, trips, departments, or jobs
• Notes field for extra context, approval details, or reimbursement comments
• Summary PDF export
• Detailed PDF export with receipt images
• CSV export for spreadsheets and bookkeeping
• Report title, submitter, company, client/project, period, and notes

Privacy matters when receipts may contain card fragments, addresses, employee names, hotel stays, meals, or client details. Expense Report Maker is designed around local-first record keeping. Your saved expenses and receipt images are stored on your device, and receipt recognition runs on device using Apple technology. You stay in control of what you save, edit, delete, export, and share.

name: GlowFeel: Stress Ease
description:
GlowFeel is a private, gentle space for slowing down when your mind feels full. Open the app for a few quiet minutes to breathe, name what you feel, write a reflection, follow a grounding exercise, or return to a small daily message that helps you feel steadier.

It is built around five clear promises: Offline, No Login, Privacy First, On Device, No Cloud Upload. GlowFeel does not ask you to create an app account before you can care for yourself. Your journal entries, emotional check-ins, grounding notes, preferences, and saved reflections are designed to stay on your iPhone. There is no cloud diary to manage and no social feed to perform for.

What you can do in GlowFeel:

* Express what you are feeling
Choose a simple emotion such as happy, sad, anxious, calm, angry, or tired, then write what is on your mind. GlowFeel offers warm, validating responses that help you pause and notice your feelings without judgment. It is not a chatbot for diagnosis, not therapy, and not medical care. It is a private place to put words around a moment.

* Breathe with calm visual rhythms
Use guided breathing exercises when your body needs a slower pace. Follow soft animations for patterns such as 4-4-6 breathing, box breathing, relaxing breath, balanced breath, and other simple rhythms. The goal is not to do it perfectly; it is to give your attention a calm shape for a few cycles.

* Ground yourself with the 5-4-3-2-1 method
When you feel scattered or overwhelmed, the grounding exercise guides you through what you can see, touch, hear, smell, and taste. This sensory check-in can help you come back to the present moment and notice the world around you in small, concrete steps.

* Journal with prompts or free writing
Write freely, or begin with thoughtful prompts like what surprised you today, what is taking up space in your mind, or how you can be gentler with yourself. Recent reflections stay easy to revisit, so you can notice patterns in your feelings without turning self-care into a complicated tracking system.

* Receive gentle daily messages
Daily Glow brings a soft reminder to your home screen inside the app. It can be a small sentence of encouragement, a moment of perspective, or a reason to take a breath before moving on with the day.

* Keep your emotional space private
Privacy matters because feelings can be deeply personal. A journal entry, an anxious thought, a grounding note, or a late-night reflection may contain details about your relationships, health, work, family, or identity. GlowFeel is designed so you can use it without handing those moments to a cloud account.

Offline: core features are made for daily use without depending on an internet account.
No Login: open the app and use expression, journaling, breathing, grounding, and settings without registration.
Privacy First: the experience is designed around calm, clarity, and user control, not tracking.
On Device: entries, exercises, reminders, appearance settings, and privacy preferences are handled on your device.
No Cloud Upload: GlowFeel does not upload your journal, expressions, grounding notes, or exercise history to its own cloud.

GlowFeel is not a crisis service and does not replace a therapist, doctor, counselor, or emergency support. It makes no clinical diagnosis and does not claim to treat any condition. It is a self-reflection and emotional wellness companion for people who want a calmer place to breathe, ground, journal, and come back to themselves.

When life feels noisy, GlowFeel gives you a quieter corner: a few breaths, a few words, a grounding step, and a private record that stays with you.

name: Fast Rhythm: Fasting & Sleep
description:
Fast Rhythm: Fasting & Sleep is a calm fasting timer and sleep rhythm tracker for people who want consistency without accounts, noise, or complicated health dashboards.

The idea is simple: fasting is easier to understand when you also pay attention to sleep. Fast Rhythm brings Fasting + Sleep + Regularity into one Metabolic Rhythm Score, so you can see whether your daily routine is moving in a steadier direction. It is built for ordinary routines: a 16:8 fast after dinner, a 14:10 plan during busy weeks, a longer fast when you feel ready, and a sleep log that helps you notice when rest is supporting or hurting your rhythm.

Core privacy promises are built into the product story: Offline, No Login, Privacy First, On Device, No Cloud Upload. You can open the app and start tracking without creating an account. Your fasting sessions, sleep records, hunger notes, quality ratings, streaks, achievements and settings are designed to stay on your iPhone.

What you can track:

* Fasting timer
Start a fast, follow elapsed time, see how much time remains, and stop when you are ready. Use common plans such as 12:12, 14:10, 16:8, 18:6 and 20:4, or choose a custom rhythm that fits your week. The timer helps you separate the fasting window from the eating window without turning every meal into a spreadsheet.

* Sleep rhythm
Log bedtime and wake time, rate sleep quality, and review sleep duration. Fast Rhythm also helps you notice sleep debt and irregular nights, because a late night often changes how a fast feels the next day. The sleep view is practical: record, wake up, rate, and move on.

* Metabolic Rhythm Score
The dashboard combines sleep quality, sleep regularity, fasting compliance and behavior synergy into one score. It is not a diagnosis and it is not medical advice. It is a simple feedback loop for habits: did you sleep enough, did you keep a reasonable rhythm, did you complete your fast, and did fasting and sleep support each other?

* Weekly rhythm report
Review a 7-day report with average sleep, average fasting time, completed fasts, daily scores, best day, areas that need attention and key factors for the week. The report is useful when you do not want to judge a routine from one good or bad day.

* Insights and recommendations
See the score breakdown and practical suggestions, such as improving sleep duration, reducing sleep debt, keeping a steady bedtime, choosing a fasting plan that matches your energy, or protecting recovery after a poor night. The goal is sustainable rhythm, not intensity for its own sake.

* Streaks, profile and achievements
Track total fasts, fasting streak, average fast duration, sleep streak, average sleep, sleep quality score, and achievements such as First Fast, 7-Day Streak, 16-Hour Club, Sleep Week and Rhythm Master. These milestones are small signals that your routine is becoming more consistent.

Privacy matters because fasting and sleep can reveal personal routines, health goals, meal timing, rest habits and lifestyle patterns. Fast Rhythm is designed to keep that information local.

Offline: core tracking, history, dashboard, reports, insights and settings are designed for use without a required online account.
No Login: no registration, username, password or social sign-in is required to start.
Privacy First: the app focuses on your daily routine, not building a cloud profile around your body clock.
On Device: fasting data, sleep data, scores, streaks, achievements and preferences are stored locally on your iPhone.
No Cloud Upload: Fast Rhythm does not upload your fasting and sleep history to its own cloud.

Use Fast Rhythm for lightweight fasting, sleep logs, weekly rhythm awareness and private on-device tracking. It supports habits, not medical treatment; ask a qualified health professional if fasting may be unsafe for you.

name: Image Compressor & ZIP
description:
Image Compressor & ZIP turns your iPhone into a fast, private photo compressor. Shrink large images in seconds, keep them visually clear, and free up storage — then export everything as a tidy ZIP file. No account, no cloud, no waiting on a server.

Compress photos quickly
Pick one photo or dozens at once and compress them in a single batch. Image Compressor reduces file size fast so your pictures are easier to share, email, upload to forms, and send to clients. Big camera photos that clog up your phone become small, lightweight files you can actually move around.

Choose your compression level
Not every photo needs the same treatment, so you stay in control. Choose a ready-made level for the smallest possible file, a recommended balance of clarity and size that fits most photos, or a sharper setting that keeps more detail for work images, travel shots, and client sharing. Prefer to fine-tune? Adjust quality and resolution yourself and switch the output format between JPEG and PNG.

Compare quality before you save
Many compressors only show the result — and you discover the photo got blurry or barely shrank after it is already saved. Image Compressor lets you compare quality before and after side by side, so you can pick a setting with confidence. A built-in quality score (PSNR / SSIM) helps you judge how close the compressed image stays to the original, instead of guessing.

See exactly how much you save
Every compression shows the original size, the compressed size, and the space you saved, clearly and up front. No vague promises — just real numbers so you know the result was worth it before you export or share.

Export as ZIP or share instantly
Bundle a whole batch into one ZIP file for clean, organized export — perfect for sending many photos at once without uploading them anywhere. Or save compressed images straight to your device, drop them into the Files app, or share them instantly through the standard iOS share sheet to Mail, Messages, and other apps.

Track your compression history
A simple history keeps your recent compression results in one place, so you can revisit how much space you saved and grab the files again when you need them. The interface stays clean and simple, focused on getting the job done without clutter.

Built for privacy
Image Compressor is designed around five clear privacy promises:

Offline: compressing, resizing, comparing, ZIP export, and saving all work without an internet connection.
No Login: open the app and start compressing — no sign-up, no account, and no password just to shrink your own photos.
Privacy First: your photos are personal, and the app treats them that way from start to finish.
On Device: image processing, resizing, quality comparison, ZIP creation, and exports all happen on your iPhone.
No Cloud Upload: your photos are never uploaded to our servers — nothing leaves your device unless you choose to share it yourself.

Great for everyday photos and real work
Use Image Compressor to shrink vacation albums before sharing, prepare lighter images for websites and email, fit photos under upload size limits on forms and job applications, send batches to clients without filling their inbox, or simply reclaim storage on a full phone. Students, freelancers, small business owners, real estate and trades workers, travelers, and anyone with too many big photos can compress, compare, and export in just a few taps.

name: Stringer: Stair Layout
description:
Stringer is a focused stair and stair-stringer calculator for builders, remodelers, deck crews, finish carpenters, makers, inspectors, and careful DIYers who want usable numbers before cutting timber. Enter the total rise and the going, choose imperial or metric units, pick the building-code reference you want to compare against, and Stringer turns the job into clear stair options you can read on site.

Privacy is built into the workflow: Offline / No Login / Privacy First / On Device / No Cloud Upload. You can open the app in a basement, garage, workshop, rural jobsite, or anywhere without reliable service. Calculations, saved projects, settings, and exported cut sheets stay on the device. There is no account to create, no sync step to wait for, and no project data being uploaded to a cloud service.

Use Stringer when you need to decide how many risers fit a stair, what each riser height should be, how many treads are needed, and what total run and pitch that choice creates. The results screen highlights a recommended option, then lists nearby alternatives so you can compare 14, 15, 16, 17, 18, or 19 risers at a glance. Each option shows the riser height, tread count, total run, pitch angle, and code status, making it easier to choose a layout that works for the space instead of guessing from a scrap of paper.

The calculator is made for real construction details. Set total rise, going or tread run, maximum available run, stair width, nosing, tread thickness, stringer stock, stock price, standard or flush construction, and whether the bottom stringer should be dropped. Switch between inches and millimetres as needed. The app also estimates board length, cut count, throat depth, first cut, and the framing-square rise and run values you need when laying out a stringer by hand.

Code references are available for quick checks against common stair rules, including IRC for US/CA work, NCC for Australia, and Doc K for the UK. Stringer can flag riser height, tread depth, pitch, stair width, and headroom notes in a readable compliance panel. These checks are intended as a field aid, not as a replacement for local code review, permits, or professional judgement. Always verify site conditions, headroom, landings, guards, handrails, and local amendments before final construction.

The visual results are designed to reduce mistakes. View the stair elevation, the full stringer shape, or the cut sheet. Dimension callouts show rise, going, total rise, total run, pitch, board length, throat depth, plumb angle, and first cut. The stringer layout view helps you see how the stair will land on the stock before you mark the board. This is useful when checking whether a 2x12 has enough throat, whether the run fits the opening, or whether a different riser count gives a better balance.

Projects help keep jobsite numbers organized. Save a project, create a new one, return to previous calculations, and keep your settings ready for the next stair. When the layout is ready, export a professional-looking Stair Cut Sheet PDF with a summary, diagram, code-compliance table, and stringer layout notes. Share it with a crew member, client, inspector, or your own future self before lumber is cut.

Stringer is intentionally simple: no login wall, no cloud workspace, no noisy collaboration feed, and no hidden web requirement. It is a practical stair layout tool for people who need fast answers, readable drawings, and repeatable numbers while keeping project information private and local.
